result.stdout:
Usage:
  abphelper [options] [command]

Options:
  --version         Show version information
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  module           Help quickly install/update/uninstall ABP modules. See 'abphelper module --help' for details
  gen, generate    Generate files for ABP projects. See 'abphelper generate --help' for details
  ef               A shortcut to run 'dotnet ef' commands. See 'abphelper ef --help' for details

commands.payload:
ef:
  A shortcut to run 'dotnet ef' commands. See 'abphelper ef --help' for details

Usage:
  abphelper ef [options] [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  migrations    run `dotnet ef migrations` command

result.stdout:
migrations:
  run `dotnet ef migrations` command

Usage:
  abphelper ef migrations [options] [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <name> <ef-options>    Adds a new migration. The usage is the same as `dotnet ef migrations add`, except providing the default value for the `--project` and `--startup-project` options.
  remove <ef-options>        Removes the last migration. The usage is the same as `dotnet ef migrations remove`, except providing the default value for the `--project` and `--startup-project` options.

commands.payload:
migrations:
  run `dotnet ef migrations` command

Usage:
  abphelper ef migrations [options] [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <name> <ef-options>    Adds a new migration. The usage is the same as `dotnet ef migrations add`, except providing the default value for the `--project` and `--startup-project` options.
  remove <ef-options>        Removes the last migration. The usage is the same as `dotnet ef migrations remove`, except providing the default value for the `--project` and `--startup-project` options.

result.stdout:
add:
  Adds a new migration. The usage is the same as `dotnet ef migrations add`, except providing the default value for the `--project` and `--startup-project` options.

Usage:
  abphelper ef migrations add [options] <name> [<ef-options>...]

Arguments:
  <name>          The name of the migration.
  <ef-options>    Other options to `dotnet ef migrations add`

Options:
  --migration-project-name <migration-project-name>    Specify the name of the migration project. For ABP applications after v4.4.0, the default value is '*.EntityFrameworkCore.csproj'; For ABP applications before v4.4.0, the default value is '*.EntityFrameworkCore.DbMigrations.csproj'; For ABP modules, the default value is '*.HttpApi.Host.csproj'. For example: --migration-project-name *.Web.Unified.csproj, abphelper will search '*.Web.Unified.csproj' file and make it as the migration project. 
  -d, --directory <directory>                          The ABP project root directory. If no directory is specified, current directory is used
  -p, --projectName <projectname>                      The ABP project name. If no name is provided, last part of project file name is used. Example: project file: 'Acme.BookStore.Domain.csproj', the name will be 'BookStore'
  --exclude <exclude>                                  Exclude directories when searching files, arguments can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters. Use double asterisk(**) to search all directories. Example: --exclude *Folder1 Folder2/Folder* **/*Folder? **/*Folder*
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

For example: --migration-project-name *.Web.Unified.csproj, abphelper will search '*.Web.Unified.csproj' file and make it as the migration project.

  -p, --projectName <projectname>                      The ABP project name. If no name is provided, last part of project file name is used. Example: project file: 'Acme.BookStore.Domain.csproj', the name will be 'BookStore'
  --exclude <exclude>                                  Exclude directories when searching files, arguments can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters. Use double asterisk(**) to search all directories. Example: --exclude *Folder1 Folder2/Folder* **/*Folder? **/*Folder*
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

controller:
  Generate controller class and methods according to the specified service

Usage:
  abphelper generate controller [options] <name>

Arguments:
  <name>    The service name(without 'AppService' or 'IntegrationService' postfix)

Options:
  --skip-build                       Skip building the solution
  -i, --integration-service          Locate the .cs files with the postfix 'IntegrationService' instead of 'AppService'
  --no-overwrite                     Specify not to overwrite existing files or content
  -d, --directory <directory>        The ABP project root directory. If no directory is specified, current directory is used
  -p, --projectName <projectname>    The ABP project name. If no name is provided, last part of project file name is used. Example: project file: 'Acme.BookStore.Domain.csproj', the name will be 'BookStore'
  --exclude <exclude>                Exclude directories when searching files, arguments can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters. Use double asterisk(**) to search all directories. Example: --exclude *Folder1 Folder2/Folder* **/*Folder? **/*Folder*
  -?, -h, --help                     Show help and usage information

'gen,' was not matched. Did you mean 'help'?
crud:
  Generate a set of CRUD related files according to the specified entity

Usage:
  abphelper generate crud [options] <entity>

Arguments:
  <entity>    The entity class name

Options:
  --migration-project-name <migration-project-name>    Specify the name of the migration project. For ABP applications after v4.4.0, the default value is '*.EntityFrameworkCore.csproj'; For ABP applications before v4.4.0, the default value is '*.EntityFrameworkCore.DbMigrations.csproj'; For ABP modules, the default value is '*.HttpApi.Host.csproj'. For example: --migration-project-name *.Web.Unified.csproj, abphelper will search '*.Web.Unified.csproj' file and make it as the migration project. This argument takes effect only if '--skip-db-migrations' is NOT specified.
  --skip-permissions                                   Skip generating crud permissions
  --skip-get-list-input-dto                            skip generate GetListInput Dto files
  --separate-dto                                       Generate separate Create and Update DTO files
  --entity-prefix-dto                                  Start DTO name with the entity name. If this option is specified, the dto name would be e.g. 'TodoCreateDto', otherwise it would be 'CreateTodoDto'
  --dto-suffix <dto-suffix>                            Use a customized DTO name suffix instead of 'Dto'
  --skip-custom-repository                             Skip generating custom repository interface and class for the entity
  --skip-db-migrations                                 Skip performing db migration and update
  --skip-ui                                            Skip generating UI
  --skip-view-model                                    Skip generating 'CreateUpdateViewModel`, use 'CreateUpdateDto' directly
  --skip-localization                                  Skip generating localization
  --skip-test                                          Skip generating test
  --skip-entity-constructors                           Skip generating constructors for the entity
  --no-overwrite                                       Specify not to overwrite existing files or content
  -d, --directory <directory>                          The ABP project root directory. If no directory is specified, current directory is used
  -p, --projectName <projectname>                      The ABP project name. If no name is provided, last part of project file name is used. Example: project file: 'Acme.BookStore.Domain.csproj', the name will be 'BookStore'
  --exclude <exclude>                                  Exclude directories when searching files, arguments can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters. Use double asterisk(**) to search all directories. Example: --exclude *Folder1 Folder2/Folder* **/*Folder? **/*Folder*
  -?, -h, --help                                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
module:
  Help quickly install/update/uninstall ABP modules. See 'abphelper module --help' for details

Usage:
  abphelper module [options] [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help    Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <module-name>       Add ABP module according to the specified packages
  remove <module-name>    Remove ABP module according to the specified packages

result.stdout:
add:
  Add ABP module according to the specified packages

Usage:
  abphelper module add [options] <module-name>

Arguments:
  <module-name>    The module name

Options:
  -v, --version <version>                        Specify the version of the package(s) to add
  --module-company-name <module-company-name>    The module company name, it will be set to the part before the first "." of the module name if you do not specify.
  -s, --shared                                   Install the {module-name}.Domain.Shared nuget package
  -o, --domain                                   Install the {module-name}.Domain nuget package
  -e, --entity-framework-core                    Install the {module-name}.EntityFrameworkCore nuget package
  -m, --mongo-db                                 Install the {module-name}.MongoDB nuget package
  -c, --contracts                                Install the {module-name}.Application.Contracts nuget package
  -a, --application                              Install the {module-name}.Application nuget package
  -h, --http-api                                 Install the {module-name}.HttpApi nuget package
  -l, --client                                   Install the {module-name}.HttpApi.Client nuget package
  -w, --web                                      Install the {module-name}.Web nuget package
  --custom <custom>                              Specify which module is added to (or removed from) which app project. e.g. "Web:Web,Orders.Web:Web:Orders" means the Web module and the Orders.Web module were added to (or removed from) the Web app project, the last optional "Orders" is the submodule name used to make up the using namespace.
  -d, --directory <directory>                    The ABP project root directory. If no directory is specified, current directory is used
  -p, --projectName <projectname>                The ABP project name. If no name is provided, last part of project file name is used. Example: project file: 'Acme.BookStore.Domain.csproj', the name will be 'BookStore'
  --exclude <exclude>                            Exclude directories when searching files, arguments can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters. Use double asterisk(**) to search all directories. Example: --exclude *Folder1 Folder2/Folder* **/*Folder? **/*Folder*
result.stderr: You must specify at least one package to add.

commands.payload:
add:
  Add ABP module according to the specified packages

Usage:
  abphelper module add [options] <module-name>

Arguments:
  <module-name>    The module name

Options:
  -v, --version <version>                        Specify the version of the package(s) to add
  --module-company-name <module-company-name>    The module company name, it will be set to the part before the first "." of the module name if you do not specify.
  -s, --shared                                   Install the {module-name}.Domain.Shared nuget package
  -o, --domain                                   Install the {module-name}.Domain nuget package
  -e, --entity-framework-core                    Install the {module-name}.EntityFrameworkCore nuget package
  -m, --mongo-db                                 Install the {module-name}.MongoDB nuget package
  -c, --contracts                                Install the {module-name}.Application.Contracts nuget package
  -a, --application                              Install the {module-name}.Application nuget package
  -h, --http-api                                 Install the {module-name}.HttpApi nuget package
  -l, --client                                   Install the {module-name}.HttpApi.Client nuget package
  -w, --web                                      Install the {module-name}.Web nuget package
  --custom <custom>                              Specify which module is added to (or removed from) which app project. e.g. "Web:Web,Orders.Web:Web:Orders" means the Web module and the Orders.Web module were added to (or removed from) the Web app project, the last optional "Orders" is the submodule name used to make up the using namespace.
  -d, --directory <directory>                    The ABP project root directory. If no directory is specified, current directory is used
  -p, --projectName <projectname>                The ABP project name. If no name is provided, last part of project file name is used. Example: project file: 'Acme.BookStore.Domain.csproj', the name will be 'BookStore'
  --exclude <exclude>                            Exclude directories when searching files, arguments can contain a combination of valid literal path and wildcard (* and ?) characters. Use double asterisk(**) to search all directories. Example: --exclude *Folder1 Folder2/Folder* **/*Folder? **/*Folder*
You must specify at least one package to add.
